name: website-ui-audit-rubric

checks.description: Visitor can identify the business category in 5 seconds.
First viewport has a strong, relevant niche visual rather than a text-heavy or generic layout.
Headline has a concrete business/value angle, not generic welcome/trusted-partner copy.
Phone, quote, booking, or contact CTA is visible and visually prioritized.
Service area or key service appears near the first viewport.
Visual/text balance feels intentional and not cramped, empty, or misaligned.
Color, type, spacing, and imagery match the niche and price tier.
Output follows the selected reference/template family without copying protected brands.
Page structure fits the business model: service, quote, appointment, project proof, or catalog.
Page does not feel like SaaS, blog, portfolio, agency, or restaurant when the niche is different.
Typography scale clearly separates H1, H2, body, labels, and buttons.
Spacing is consistent and not randomly cramped or overly empty.
Sections have a natural scanning rhythm and do not all look identical.
Contrast and palette support readability, trust, and brand tone.
No repeated headers, duplicated facts, or filler modules that add noise.
Photos clearly relate to the niche, services, materials, or local business promise.
Images are high-resolution, well-cropped, and not visually broken.
Image set supports hero, service, proof, and contact contexts without looking repetitive.
AI images do not show obvious impossible anatomy, warped tools, broken text, or fake logos.
Generated, searched, official, or placeholder image source is recorded.
Primary action is obvious and repeated at sensible moments.
Phone, form, email, map, or contact page path is present and clickable when relevant.
Page gives a reason to trust: process, reviews, project proof, guarantee policy, credentials, or transparent placeholder proof.
Copy/UI reduces hesitation: free quote, inspection, response time, what happens next.
Mobile first viewport still communicates business, value, and CTA.
No horizontal scrolling, overlap, clipped text, or broken columns.
Buttons and links are large enough and not crowded.
Important sections appear in a useful order on mobile.
Images crop gracefully on mobile.
Key links and forms are clickable and not dead placeholders unless clearly marked.
Text contrast, semantic headings, alt text, and focus states are acceptable for demo quality.
No visible runtime errors or missing asset failures.
No internal workflow, audit, prompt, artifact, readiness, or generated labels leak into the website.
Avoids generic AI layout clichés, vague gradients, generic trust copy, and bland repeated cards.
Demo-only claims are clearly placeholders and not presented as verified truth.

rerun.when: hard fail caused by wrong direction, weak hero, missing visual assets, or generic unusable layout

human_review.when: confidence is low, evidence conflicts, or two revision rounds fail

hardFailRules: Hero is visually weak, text-heavy, or not industry-specific.
Contact path is missing or broken.
Internal workflow text appears in customer-facing UI.
Fake exact claims are presented as true facts.
Major mobile layout break or unreadable content.
Open Design did not reach native clean finish but result is being considered for approval.
Reference family is ignored enough to change the intended template direction.
